# grafana_assistant_mcp_server (Resource)

Manages a Grafana Assistant MCP server integration.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "grafana_assistant_mcp_server" "example" {
  name         = "Example MCP server"
  scope        = "tenant"
  applications = ["assistant"]

  configuration {
    url = "https://example.com/mcp/"
  }

  custom_headers = {
    Authorization = "Bearer ${var.mcp_token}"
  }
}

variable "mcp_token" {
  type      = string
  sensitive = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The MCP server integration name.
- `scope` (String) Whether the resource is visible to the whole tenant (`tenant`) or only the creating user (`user`).

### Optional

- `applications` (List of String) Applications where this resource applies. Valid values: `assistant`, `loop`, `all`. Defaults to all applications when unset.
- `configuration` (Block, Optional) MCP server configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--configuration))
- `custom_headers` (Map of String, Sensitive) Custom HTTP headers sent to the MCP server. Values are write-only and not returned by the API.
- `description` (String) Optional description.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the resource is enabled.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The UUID of the resource.

<a id="nestedblock--configuration"></a>
### Nested Schema for `configuration`

Optional:

- `builtin_id` (String) Built-in provider ID (e.g. cursor). When set, tools are provided locally.
- `tool_approval_policies` (Map of String) Tool approval policies keyed by tool name (`auto_approve`, `always_ask`, or empty for default).
- `tool_preferences` (Map of String) Tool preferences keyed by tool name (`enabled` or `disabled`).
- `url` (String) MCP server URL.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
terraform import grafana_assistant_mcp_server.name "{{ id }}"
```
